evidence: The pipeline from scripts -> monte_carlo -> tier1_rollout -> hypatia_adapter has clear, explicit data contracts using frozen dataclasses (Tier1RolloutConfig, Tier1MonteCarloConfig, Tier1RunRow). Config-to-config translation from MonteCarloConfig to RolloutConfig happens in one obvious place (generate_tier1_temporal_dataset).

The failure application logic (copy graph, remove failed nodes, then remove failed edges) is duplicated between tier1_rollout.py (lines 327-335) and gnn_dataset.py (lines 259-264). This is a boundary translation that should happen in one canonical place but instead exists in two modules with subtly different code paths (tier1_rollout uses sorted-tuple edge matching; gnn_dataset does direct removal). Divergence risk is real since both must produce identical effective graphs for labels to match regenerated data.

All three export/sweep scripts (export_design_dataset.py, export_failure_dataset.py, failure_sweep.py) use identical sys.path manipulation boilerplate to resolve the src/ import path. This is ad-hoc glue-code at the script-to-library boundary that should be handled by proper package installation (pip install -e .).
The models/__init__.py lazy-import pattern provides a clean integration seam that avoids requiring torch/torch_geometric for non-ML consumers. This is a good boundary design.

The legacy quarantine pattern in legacy/__init__.py is well-documented with clear deprecation notices and explicit pointers to replacement modules, making the seam between legacy and production code predictable.

The Tier1FailureRealization class provides clean serialization/deserialization (to_json_strings/from_json_strings) bridging the simulation-to-CSV-to-ML boundary. However, the consuming side in gnn_dataset.py re-implements failure application rather than calling a shared function, creating a parallel boundary translation.

issues.summary: Failure application logic duplicated across tier1_rollout.py and gnn_dataset.py with subtle divergence risk
evidence: tier1_rollout.py lines 327-335 apply failures: copy graph, remove failed nodes, remove failed edges with sorted-tuple edge matching
gnn_dataset.py lines 259-264 re-implement the same logic: copy graph, remove failed nodes, remove failed edges via direct removal
Both must produce identical effective graphs for the 'Graph Reconstruction Contract (Step 3)' to hold, but the logic is not shared
issues.suggestion: Extract a shared function like apply_persistent_failures(G, failures: Tier1FailureRealization) -> nx.Graph into tier1_rollout.py or a dedicated utility, and call it from both tier1_rollout.run_tier1_rollout() and gnn_dataset.SatNetTemporalDataset.get(). This ensures the reconstruction contract is enforced by a single code path.

issues.summary: Three scripts duplicate identical sys.path manipulation boilerplate to resolve src/ imports
evidence: export_design_dataset.py lines 17-21: PROJECT_ROOT / 'src' path manipulation with sys.path.insert
scripts/train_gnn_model.py and scripts/train_design_risk_model.py also use the same pattern
issues.suggestion: Add a pyproject.toml or setup.py with 'pip install -e .' so that satnet is importable without sys.path manipulation. This eliminates the ad-hoc glue-code at the script-to-library boundary and lets all scripts use clean imports.

root_causes: The failure-application duplication stems from the gnn_dataset module needing to reconstruct graphs independently of the rollout pipeline, but no shared utility was extracted when the reconstruction contract was established.
The sys.path boilerplate in scripts is a symptom of the project not having a proper installable package configuration.
